bins.name: Cost allocation and rate fairness
bins.desc: Senators oppose spreading data center transmission costs to residential ratepayers and urge FERC to reject filings that do not properly allocate costs. They cite PJM's tripled capacity auction costs and call for proactive investigation into discriminatory utility practices.
quotes: PJM's 2024 capacity market auction, which aims to secure resources to guarantee that the grid will be able to meet peak demand in the coming months, closed at a record high of $16.6 billion, up from just $2.2 billion in 2023.

In order to protect ratepayers from unreasonable or unjust price hikes driven by data center energy demand, FERC should reject transmission-related filings that do not properly address cost allocation or allow the cost of transmission built to serve data centers to be spread across the region.

Beyond addressing proposals as they are presented to FERC, the Commission must proactively investigate and act if it finds the practices of a regional operator or utility to be unjust, unreasonable, unduly discriminatory, or preferential to data centers at the expense of residential ratepayers.

As FERC considers Energy Secretary Chris Wright's recent request for a rulemaking to standardize and accelerate the interconnection of large loads like data centers, We urge FERC to prioritize the concerns of residential ratepayers.

bins.name: Load forecasting transparency and standardization
bins.desc: Senators support standardizing load forecasting and increasing transparency into data center demand projections. They note that uncertain and speculative projections are driving billions in utility investments.
quotes: Prompted by projections like these – many of which rely on uncertain and speculative data center proposals – utilities and transmission planning regions are spending billions to guarantee the grid can meet this demand.
FERC should also consider broader proceedings that would standardize load forecasting and other practices relevant to addressing data center demand growth.

Without transparency into load forecasting, it is difficult to assess the quality of a utility's load projections, making it challenging to ensure the economically responsible build-out of new generation or transmission assets.

bins.name: PJM-specific concerns
bins.desc: Senators highlight PJM capacity auction costs that have tripled and note that data centers will drive 90 percent of new demand over the next five years.
quotes: PJM's 2024 capacity market auction, which aims to secure resources to guarantee that the grid will be able to meet peak demand in the coming months, closed at a record high of $16.6 billion, up from just $2.2 billion in 2023.

In PJM Interconnection, the regional transmission organization (RTO) that serves thirteen states and the District of Columbia in the mid-Atlantic region, data centers will drive 90% of new power demand over the next five years.

bins.name: Data center electricity demand growth
bins.desc: Filing documents rapid growth in AI and cryptomining electricity demand that utilities must plan for and that significantly impacts ratepayers.
quotes: Artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptomining are fueling a rising demand for energy driven by massive, energy-intensive data centers. The resulting surge in electricity use is contributing to skyrocketing residential electric bills.

In PJM Interconnection, the regional transmission organization (RTO) that serves thirteen states and the District of Columbia in the mid-Atlantic region, data centers will drive 90% of new power demand over the next five years.

bins.name: Uncertain data center demand projections
bins.desc: Senators warn that utilities are investing billions based on uncertain and speculative data center proposals that may not materialize.
quotes: Prompted by projections like these – many of which rely on uncertain and speculative data center proposals – utilities and transmission planning regions are spending billions to guarantee the grid can meet this demand.

With a guaranteed rate of return of around 11%, there is an obvious incentive for utilities to push for huge investments in transmission, including transmission for speculative data center interconnection requests that may not materialize.

bins.name: Need for transparent load forecasting
bins.desc: Transparent load forecasting is necessary to assess utility projections and ensure economically responsible transmission and generation investments.
quotes: Without transparency into load forecasting, it is difficult to assess the quality of a utility's load projections, making it challenging to ensure the economically responsible build-out of new generation or transmission assets.

bins.name: Utility rate-of-return incentives
bins.desc: Senators identify the 11 percent guaranteed rate of return as creating incentive for utilities to overinvest in speculative data center transmission.
quotes: With a guaranteed rate of return of around 11%, there is an obvious incentive for utilities to push for huge investments in transmission, including transmission for speculative data center interconnection requests that may not materialize.

bins.name: Interconnection queue prioritization
bins.desc: Senators oppose prioritizing fossil fuel generators in interconnection queues based on data center demand projections.
quotes: Nor should FERC fall for the argument that data center demand projections must allow large fossil fuel generators to jump the queue for interconnection to the nation's energy grid.

bins.name: Renewable energy and storage priority
bins.desc: Senators support meeting near-term capacity shortfalls with faster-deploying renewable technologies and battery storage rather than fossil fuel generation.
quotes: Near-term capacity shortfalls are better met by faster-to-deploy renewable technologies and battery storage than by large, polluting, and expensive fossil fuel generation that takes years to build and get online.
